text:
To determine how many times Sandra needs to babysit to have enough eggs for 15 Spanish flans, we first need to calculate the total number of eggs required. Since each Spanish flan requires 3 eggs, we can multiply the number of flans by the number of eggs needed per flan:

\[ 15 \text{ flans} \times 3 \text{ eggs per flan} = 45 \text{ eggs} \]

Next, we need to figure out how many times Sandra needs to babysit to get 45 eggs. Since she gets 9 eggs each time she babysits, we divide the total number of eggs needed by the number of eggs she receives per babysitting session:

\[ \frac{45 \text{ eggs}}{9 \text{ eggs per babysitting session}} = 5 \text{ babysitting sessions} \]

Therefore, Sandra needs to babysit 5 times to have enough eggs to make 15 Spanish flans.
